commit.message:
fix: clip terminal content at right edge to prevent column overflow (#100)

xterm.js v6 intentionally leaves overflow:hidden commented out on its
ScrollableElement wrapper (to allow decorations to escape). Combined with
FitAddon subtracting a hardcoded 14px DEFAULT_SCROLL_BAR_WIDTH that may
not match the native Windows scrollbar width, the terminal can render ~1
column wider than the visible area — producing orphaned characters at the
right edge.

Fix:
- Add overflow:hidden on .terminal-surface and .xterm to clip stray pixels
- Hide the native scrollbar on .xterm-viewport (the custom
  SmoothScrollableElement scrollbar handles all interaction)
- Apply same fix to analysis-window__terminal for consistency

commit.message:
network: event-driven flood defense — Notify enforcement, token bucket gate, kick_peer

- AttackDetector: add ban_notify (Arc<Notify>); fire notify_one() on every
  new (non-duplicate) attack pattern; expose via ban_notifier()
- AI enforcement loop: replace 30s sleep with tokio::select!{notify, sleep(30s)}
  so bans are applied immediately on detection, not up to 30s later
- Enforcement loop: collect IPs to kick into Vec, drop blacklist lock, then
  call kick_peer().await — bans now actually close TCP connections instantly
  instead of only being checked on the next message via mark_disconnected
- Token bucket flood gate (TLS + plaintext bridges): replace 1-second window
  counter (gate_count/gate_window) with an event-driven token bucket that
  refills proportionally to elapsed time between messages; no polling timer
  needed; 300 consecutive soft-drops still triggers hard disconnect
- msg_read channel: unbounded_channel -> channel(512) for per-peer backpressure;
  bridge tasks use send().await so TCP window fills up and signals the remote
  sender to slow down when handler is busy
- kick_peer() added to all should_ban break paths in handle_peer:
  rate_limit_check! macro, invalid transaction, corrupted block (×2),
  ping flood escalation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: store harvestableAt to eliminate tooltip/harvest timing bugs

Root causes identified and fixed:

1. Race condition: tooltip computed effectiveDuration dynamically on
   every hover while tickCrops only ran every 1s. When remaining time
   hit 0 in the tooltip, plot.status was still 'growing' so clicking
   did nothing for up to 1 second.

2. Season change jumps: both tooltip and tickCrops recalculated
   effectiveDuration from the *current* season each time. A season
   change would retroactively alter the duration — e.g. tooltip
   showing '5s remaining' could jump to '80s remaining' or '0s' when
   a new season loaded, creating either a jarring UX jump or instantly
   triggering the race condition.

3. Windmill/greenhouse same pattern: placing or selling a building
   changed effectiveDuration mid-growth with no stable timestamp to
   compare against.

Fix: add harvestableAt (absolute ms timestamp) to PlotState. It is
computed once at plant time based on the current season and buildings,
then recomputed when windmill or greenhouse is placed/sold (barn has
no grow-duration effect). tickCrops and the tooltip both read from
this single timestamp — no more dynamic recalculation divergence.

Additional changes:
- harvestPlot and harvestMulti now also accept status='growing' when
  harvestableAt has elapsed (eliminates the 1s click-dead-zone)
- onTileClick updated with the same condition
- Old saves migrated: harvestableAt computed from plantedAt on load
- Unused SEASON_CONFIGS/getAdjustedGrowDuration imports removed from
  GameCanvas.tsx

* Video Indexer, Multi-Modal Enhancements, Scope Bug

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled
- Validate image upload UI shows extracted text and vision analysis
- Confirm conversation history properly handles image context without token overflow

### 🔐 Security & Performance
- Managed identity authentication improves security posture (no stored API keys)
- Image base64 data excluded from conversation history prevents token exhaustion
- Metadata citations add minimal overhead while providing rich context
- Vision analysis runs efficiently during document processing pipeline

### 📝 Configuration Required
Admins must configure:
1. Enable `enable_multimodal_vision` in admin settings
2. Select vision-capable model (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision-preview`)
3. For Video Indexer: Configure managed identity and ARM resource details
4. Enable `enable_extract_meta_data` to surface metadata citations

## PR Summary: Video Indexer Multi-Modal Enhancements

### Overview
This PR introduces significant enhancements to video processing and image analysis capabilities, focusing on multi-modal AI features and improved metadata handling. **Version updated from 0.233.167 to 0.233.172**.

### 🎯 Key Features

#### 1. **Multi-Modal Vision Analysis for Images**
- Added AI-powered vision analysis for uploaded images using GPT-4 Vision or similar models
- Extracts comprehensive image insights including:
  - AI-generated descriptions
  - Object detection
  - Text extraction from images (OCR)
  - Detailed visual analysis
- New admin setting: `enable_multimodal_vision` to control feature availability
- Vision analysis results stored in document metadata and included in AI Search indexing
- Connection testing endpoint added for vision model validation

#### 2. **Enhanced Document Metadata Citations**
- Implemented metadata-based citations that surface document keywords, abstracts, and vision analysis
- New citation types displayed with distinct visual indicators:
  - **Keywords**: Tagged with `bi-tags` icon, labeled as "Metadata"
  - **Abstract**: Document summaries included as contextual citations
  - **Vision Analysis**: AI-generated image insights labeled as "AI Vision"
- Metadata content passed to AI models as additional context for more informed responses
- Special modal view for metadata citations (separate from standard document citations)

#### 3. **Image Message UI Improvements**
- Enhanced display for user-uploaded images vs AI-generated images
- Added "View Text" button for uploaded images with extracted content or vision analysis
- Collapsible info sections showing:
  - Extracted OCR text from Document Intelligence
  - AI Vision Analysis results
- Proper avatar distinction between uploaded and generated images
- Improved metadata tracking with `is_user_upload` flag

#### 4. **Video Indexer Configuration Updates**
- **BREAKING CHANGE**: Removed API key authentication support
- Now exclusively uses **Managed Identity authentication** for Video Indexer
- Updated admin UI documentation to guide managed identity setup:
  - Enable system-assigned managed identity on App Service
  - Assign "Video Indexer Restricted Viewer" role
  - Configure required ARM settings (subscription ID, resource group, account name)
- Improved validation for required Video Indexer settings
- Enhanced error messaging for missing configuration

#### 5. **Search Scope Improvements**
- Fixed search behavior when `document_scope='all'` to properly include group documents
- Added `active_group_id` to search context when document scope is 'all' and groups are enabled
- Conditional group index searching - only queries group index when `active_group_id` is present
- Prevents unnecessary searches and potential errors when groups aren't in use

#### 6. **Image Context in Conversation History**
- Enhanced conversation history to include rich image context for AI models
- Extracts and includes:
  - OCR text from Document Intelligence (up to max content length)
  - AI Vision analysis (description, objects, text)
  - Structured prompt formatting for multimodal understanding
- **Important**: Base64 image data excluded from conversation history to prevent token overflow
- Only metadata and extracted insights passed to models for efficient token usage

### 🔧 Technical Improvements

#### Backend Changes
- **route_backend_chats.py**: 
  - Added metadata citation extraction logic (~150 lines)
  - Enhanced conversation history building for image uploads
  - Improved search argument handling for group contexts
  
- **functions_documents.py**:
  - New `analyze_image_with_vision_model()` function for AI vision analysis
  - Enhanced `get_document_metadata_for_citations()` integration
  - Vision analysis now runs BEFORE chunk saving to include insights in AI Search indexing
  - Removed redundant blob storage for vision JSON (stored in document metadata)
  
- **route_backend_settings.py**:
  - New `_test_multimodal_vision_connection()` endpoint for testing vision models
  - Supports both APIM and direct Azure OpenAI endpoints
  - Test uses 1x1 pixel sample image for validation

- **functions_search.py**:
  - Added conditional logic for group search execution
  - Prevents empty `active_group_id` from causing search errors

#### Frontend Changes
- **chat-messages.js** (~275 lines changed):
  - Enhanced `appendMessage()` to handle uploaded image metadata
  - New `toggleImageInfo()` functionality for expandable image details
  - Improved citation rendering with metadata type indicators
  - Debug logging for image message processing
  
- **chat-citations.js** (~70 lines added):
  - New `showMetadataModal()` function for displaying keywords/abstracts/vision analysis
  - Enhanced citation click handling to detect metadata citations
  - Separate modal styling and behavior for metadata vs document citations

- **admin_settings.html**:
  - Complete redesign of Video Indexer configuration section
  - Removed all API key references
  - Added managed identity setup instructions with step-by-step guidance
  - Updated configuration display to show resource group and subscription ID

- **_video_indexer_info.html**:
  - Updated modal content to clarify managed identity requirement
  - Added warning banner about authentication type
  - Enhanced configuration display with ARM resource details

### 📊 Files Changed
- **16 files** modified
- **+1,063 insertions**, **-412 deletions**
- Net change: **+651 lines**

### 🧪 Testing Considerations
- Test multi-modal vision analysis with various image types
- Validate metadata citations appear correctly in chat responses
- Verify Video Indexer works with managed identity authentication
- Test search scope behavior with and without groups enabled

Bulk actions available:

Auto-exit after 30 seconds of inactivity

perf(maglev): inline x86 context slot access for Smi values

Eliminates FFI calls for LoadCurrentContextSlot and
StoreCurrentContextSlot when the slot contains a Smi value.
Uses compile-time layout probing of RefCell<JsContext> to
determine Vec data pointer and Smi payload offsets, then
emits direct x86 memory loads/stores (5-7 instructions vs
~30+ for an FFI call). Non-Smi values fall back to the
existing runtime stub. Critical for closure_counter where
every invocation does 1 load + 1 store of a Smi counter.

Fix race condition in GetAsync between terminal status and persistence (#57899)

* Fix race condition in GetAsync between terminal status and persistence

When a background response reaches terminal status during event processing,
GetAsync could return the completed snapshot before FinalizeExecutionAsync
called UpdateResponseAsync. Add a wait gate in GetAsync that awaits the
ExecutionTask when status is terminal but CompletedAt is null.

Fixes #57893

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix flaky test: BgMode_ProviderHasOneCreateAndOneUpdate race condition

GET returning a terminal status does not guarantee that finalization
(persistence via UpdateResponseAsync) has completed. Add a poll helper
that waits for the expected spy call before asserting, making the test
resilient to the race between observable status and persistence.

Fixes #57893

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert product code change — move fix to test level

The service team confirmed GetAsync behavior is by design. The test
must be resilient to the race between observable status and persistence
rather than gating GetAsync on finalization completion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(maglev): merge array growth + IC update into single FFI call

When a keyed store exceeds the cached IC length, the slow path
previously made 2 Rust calls: inline_store_keyed_smi (MISS) then
sta_keyed_property_with_ic (store + IC update). This merges both
into a single jit_runtime_inline_store_keyed_grow_ic_r15 that
handles array growth, stores the value, and refreshes IC slots
in one call — halving per-growth-store FFI overhead.

commit.message:
fix: recompile agentic workflows against current frontmatter (#38056)

PR #38054 compiled lock files against stale `.md` files that didn't
include the trigger changes from #38035 (`pull_request_target` →
`pull_request`). This caused the frontmatter hash stored in the lock
files to mismatch what the runtime checker computes, failing all agentic
workflows with:

```
ERR_CONFIG: Lock file is outdated! The workflow file frontmatter has changed.
```

This PR recompiles all lock files from the current `.md` files so the
hashes match.

### Packages impacted

None — workflow/CI changes only.

commit.message:
rust sdk: change download progress callback from &str to f64 (#608)

Change the public download progress callback type from FnMut(&str) to
FnMut(f64) for cross-SDK consistency. Python, JS, and C# SDKs all expose
a numeric float type for download progress.

The string-to-float parsing now happens inside model_variant.rs (using
chunk.parse::<f64>()), matching the pattern already used by
download_and_register_eps_with_progress. Unparseable chunks are silently
TryParse). The internal core_interop streaming infrastructure remains
unchanged since it is shared with chat/audio streaming.

---------

Add --include-postgres-system-objects CLI flag

When not set (default), the tool refuses to compare tables in PostgreSQL
system schemas (pg_catalog, pg_toast, pg_temp, information_schema) and
exits with an error message. When set, system schema tables are allowed.

This makes the safety guard consistent with pg_deploy and pg-extract-schema.
